title: excoecaria dallachyana
intro: Excoecaria dallachyana, also known as the native poison tree or dallachya tree, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its shiny green leaves and vibrant yellow or red flowers. The plant contains toxic compounds, particularly phorbol esters, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Excoecaria dallachyana include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. In herbal preparations, Excoecaria dallachyana is often used in the form of extracts, tinctures, or ointments, although it is essential to note that its use should be supervised by a healthcare professional due to its toxic properties.
